next_tickets.body: Question: do external boosted-tree libraries or a tiny transformer change any BAKEOFF01 recommendation where tree/NN methods are close? Reuse the BAKEOFF01 raw ROOT gate, identical run splits, and bootstrap CIs; run only XGBoost/LightGBM and a compact transformer on tasks whose top two CI bands overlap. Expected information gain: determines whether the default algorithm table is stable before broad citation.
next_tickets.title: BAKEOFF02: XGBoost/LightGBM and compact transformer near-tie audit

scientific_summary: Timing point-estimate winner is gradient_boosted_trees at 1.127 ns versus analytic_timewalk 1.495 ns. Duplicate-readout amplitude winner is random_forest at fractional res68 0.0035; charge winner is random_forest at 0.0071. Two-pulse point-estimate winner is gradient_boosted_trees at 6.996 ns versus constrained_template_fit 13.152 ns. Injected-truth anomaly winner is hist_gradient_boosting at ROC AUC 0.868. The winner named here is the held-out metric winner; adoption remains conditional on the failure-rate and leakage guards documented in REPORT.md.
